Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for wire fraud, Pham, Denise, SAR, Barbara, and Carlos-related content using search_emails to discover the five injected emails: (a) Barbara Hensley's confirmation that the spoofed domain was tryontltle.com and that Tryon Title's legitimate domain is tryontitle.com (email_inject_barbara_confirm_01), (b) Carlos Rivera's update that the borrower's bank froze approximately $12,000 of the $47,000 wire and the remaining $35,000 appears unrecoverable (email_inject_carlos_borrower_update), (c) the second spoofed email from closing@piedmontsetttlement.com with fake wire instructions for a different closing (email_inject_spoof_piedmont_01), (d) Denise Holloway's urgent SAR documentation request with an April 29 deadline listing what she needs (email_inject_denise_sar_request), and (e) Megan Sloane's guidance from Ward Barrett LLP recommending the SAR be reframed as a pattern of business email compromise and listing what the processor must prepare (email_inject_counsel_sar_guidance).
OE 2: Search Slack messages for wire fraud, Pham, recovery, or compliance-related content using conversations_search_messages to discover messages in the #compliance-alerts channel (C004) and Grace Yamamoto's group DM channel (D_grace_yamamoto, a multi-party DM). Key messages include: (a) Grace's DM telling Sofia the bank froze about $12K before it cleared and $35K is still out, asking Sofia to handle the LOS update and pull the email timeline (ts=1777040400.000000 in D_grace_yamamoto), (b) Sofia's flag in #compliance-alerts about the second spoofed email from piedmontsetttlement.com with triple-t (ts=1777042200.000000 in C004), (c) Denise's response in #compliance-alerts confirming a pattern and instructing Sofia to forward headers to Raj (ts=1777043100.000000 in C004), and (d) Denise's SAR deadline reminder referencing the April 29 filing date (ts=1777299300.000000 in C004).
OE 3: Look up the Pham loan in the mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query "Pham" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number "LN-2026-00605") to retrieve loan details: loan ID los_loan_e0934521fda4, property address 3781 Park Rd Charlotte NC 28216, status clear_to_close, borrower_id los_borrower_d679dee523c0.
OE 4: Check the activity log on loan los_loan_e0934521fda4 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log to find Carlos Rivera's existing activity note (activity_inject_carlos_pham_call) documenting his April 21 conversation with the borrower about the partial wire recovery ($12,000 frozen, $35,000 unrecovered) and the borrower maintaining HOI during the delay.
OE 5: Add an activity note to loan los_loan_e0934521fda4 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id "los_loan_e0934521fda4", action "note", author "Sofia Reyes" or similar) with detail documenting the wire fraud recovery status update, partial recovery of approximately $12,000 frozen by intermediary bank, $35,000 unrecoverable, compliance review in progress for SAR filing, and reference to the $1,500 Grayson & Associates legal bill (GA-2026-0418) tracked against this loan.
OE 6: Search mortgage LOS pipeline or loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline or mortgage_los_search_loans to identify closings that overlapped with both incident timeframes (around March 25 for the original wire fraud and around April 23 for the second spoofed email) to compile the list of potentially targeted closings that Denise and counsel need for the expanded SAR filing.
OE 7: Send an email (via send_email) to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com from sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com with details about the second spoofed email: sender domain piedmontsetttlement.com (triple-t), received April 23, fake wire instructions referencing a different closing, same pattern as the original Tryon Title spoof, so IT can trace the source and analyze email headers.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for bills related to legal counsel or wire fraud using quickbooks_search_bills (e.g., criteria referencing "Grayson" or "wire fraud" or "legal" or "LN-2026-00605") to find the existing $1,500 bill from Grayson & Associates (Id: qb_bill_inject_grayson_wirefraud, DocNumber: GA-2026-0418, VendorRef: V-GRAYSON) for wire fraud incident response and SAR preparation, dated April 15, 2026.
OE 9: Send an email (via send_email) to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com from sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com with the incident report covering: (a) timeline of the original March 25 wire fraud involving the spoofed domain tryontltle.com and the $47,000 wire diverted to a fraudulent account, (b) the second April 23 spoofed email from piedmontsetttlement.com targeting a different closing, (c) confirmation that no Keystone email accounts or systems were compromised and the attack was purely a vendor domain spoof confirmed by Tryon Title's IT vendor, (d) current recovery status ($12,000 frozen by intermediary bank, $35,000 unrecoverable), and (e) a list of closings that overlapped with both incident timeframes.
OE 10: Create a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement) with engagement_type "NOTE", contact_ids including "crm_crmcontact_5611341df3bc" (Destiny Pham) or an equivalent Destiny Pham contact record, and body documenting the wire fraud incidents, partial recovery status, SAR filing in progress, and compliance trail so the compliance team has a permanent record tied to the borrower.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search emails for wire fraud, Pham, Denise, SAR, Barbara, and Carlos-related content using search_emails to discover the five injected emails: (a) Barbara Hensley's confirmation that the spoofed domain was tryontltle.com and that Tryon Title's legitimate domain is tryontitle.com (email_inject_barbara_confirm_01), (b) Carlos Rivera's update that the borrower's bank froze approximately $12,000 of the $47,000 wire and the remaining $35,000 appears unrecoverable (email_inject_carlos_borrower_update), (c) the second spoofed email from closing@piedmontsetttlement.com with fake wire instructions for a different closing (email_inject_spoof_piedmont_01), (d) Denise Holloway's urgent SAR documentation request with an April 29 deadline listing what she needs (email_inject_denise_sar_request), and (e) Megan Sloane's guidance from Ward Barrett LLP recommending the SAR be reframed as a pattern of business email compromise and listing what the processor must prepare (email_inject_counsel_sar_guidance).
OE 2: Search Slack messages for wire fraud, Pham, recovery, or compliance-related content using conversations_search_messages to discover messages in the #compliance-alerts channel (C004) and Grace Yamamoto's group DM channel (D_grace_yamamoto, a multi-party DM). Key messages include: (a) Grace's DM telling Sofia the bank froze about $12K before it cleared and $35K is still out, asking Sofia to handle the LOS update and pull the email timeline (ts=1777040400.000000 in D_grace_yamamoto), (b) Sofia's flag in #compliance-alerts about the second spoofed email from piedmontsetttlement.com with triple-t (ts=1777042200.000000 in C004), (c) Denise's response in #compliance-alerts confirming a pattern and instructing Sofia to forward headers to Raj (ts=1777043100.000000 in C004), and (d) Denise's SAR deadline reminder referencing the April 29 filing date (ts=1777299300.000000 in C004).
OE 3: Look up the Pham loan in the mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans (query "Pham" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number "LN-2026-00605") to retrieve loan details: loan ID los_loan_e0934521fda4, property address 3781 Park Rd Charlotte NC 28216, status clear_to_close, borrower_id los_borrower_d679dee523c0.
OE 4: Check the activity log on loan los_loan_e0934521fda4 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log to find Carlos Rivera's existing activity note (activity_inject_carlos_pham_call) documenting his April 21 conversation with the borrower about the partial wire recovery ($12,000 frozen, $35,000 unrecovered) and the borrower maintaining HOI during the delay.
OE 5: Add an activity note to loan los_loan_e0934521fda4 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id "los_loan_e0934521fda4", action "note", author "Sofia Reyes" or similar) with detail documenting the wire fraud recovery status update, partial recovery of approximately $12,000 frozen by intermediary bank, $35,000 unrecoverable, compliance review in progress for SAR filing, and reference to the $1,500 Grayson & Associates legal bill (GA-2026-0418) tracked against this loan.
OE 6: Search mortgage LOS pipeline or loans using mortgage_los_get_pipeline or mortgage_los_search_loans to identify closings that overlapped with both incident timeframes (around March 25 for the original wire fraud and around April 23 for the second spoofed email) to compile the list of potentially targeted closings that Denise and counsel need for the expanded SAR filing.

OE 8: Search QuickBooks for bills related to legal counsel or wire fraud using quickbooks_search_bills (e.g., criteria referencing "Grayson" or "wire fraud" or "legal" or "LN-2026-00605") to find the existing $1,500 bill from Grayson & Associates (Id: qb_bill_inject_grayson_wirefraud, DocNumber: GA-2026-0418, VendorRef: V-GRAYSON) for wire fraud incident response and SAR preparation, dated April 15, 2026.
OE 9: Send an email (via send_email) to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com from sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com with the incident report covering: (a) timeline of the original March 25 wire fraud involving the spoofed domain tryontltle.com and the $47,000 wire diverted to a fraudulent account, (b) the second April 23 spoofed email from piedmontsetttlement.com targeting a different closing, (c) confirmation that no Keystone email accounts or systems were compromised and the attack was purely a vendor domain spoof confirmed by Tryon Title's IT vendor, (d) current recovery status ($12,000 frozen by intermediary bank, $35,000 unrecoverable), and (e) a list of closings that overlapped with both incident timeframes.

1 The model must use a mortgage_los tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, or mortgage_los_get_activity_log) to look up the Pham loan file (LN-2026-00605).
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans with query 'Pham' and later mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4'. It also called mortgage_los_get_activity_log on the same loan. All returned results confirming the loan was found.
✓Run #2The agent called both mortgage_los_search_loans (query: 'Pham') and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: 'los_loan_e0934521fda4') to look up the Pham loan file.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans with query 'Pham' and mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number 'LN-2026-00605', satisfying the requirement to look up the Pham loan file.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number 'LN-2026-00605' and also mortgage_los_get_activity_log with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4', successfully retrieving the Pham loan file and its activity log.
✓Run #5The agent called both mcp__mcp_3359ed46__mortgage_los_search_borrowers with query 'Pham' and mcp__mcp_3359ed46__mortgage_los_search_loans with query 'Pham' early in the trajectory, and later called mortgage_los_get_activity_log on loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4'.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans with query 'Pham' and then mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4', successfully retrieving the Pham loan file.
2 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills, quickbooks_search_vendors, or quickbooks_show_data) to search for the legal counsel bill related to the wire fraud.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_bills twice — once with fetchAll:true and once with criteria TotalAmt > 0 with fetchAll:true. Both returned 0 results, but the tool was used appropriately to search for legal bills.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true and also quickbooks_show_data, both of which are QuickBooks search tools targeting bill data.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills multiple times (with VendorRef LIKE %Ward%, TotalAmt > 0, fetchAll:true) and also quickbooks_show_data. QuickBooks tools were used to search for the legal counsel bill.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true and quickbooks_show_data to search for existing bills. Both returned empty results, but the tool was used.
✓Run #5The agent called mcp__mcp_3359ed46__quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true to search for existing bills. It also searched quickbooks_search_vendors multiple times. A QuickBooks search for bills was performed.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true, quickbooks_search_bills with criteria TotalAmt>0, and quickbooks_show_data — all QuickBooks tool calls related to finding bills.
3 The agent searched Slack DMs with Grace Yamamoto (querying channel D_grace_yamamoto, or using filter_in_im_or_mpim, or using a search query mentioning Grace and recovery or wire or frozen) to find Grace's message about the exact recovery numbers ($12K frozen, $35K out).
query construction
 ✗✓✗✗✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The agent searched conversations_search_messages with queries like 'Pham recovery', 'wire fraud recovery', 'recovery partial frozen', 'frozen bank recovery', '$12,000', and also with filter_users_from '@grace.yamamoto' and query 'Pham'. None returned results. The agent never directly accessed channel D_grace_yamamoto or used a search query combining Grace with 'frozen' or 'recovery' specifically. The recovery numbers were obtained from the Carlos activity note in the LOS, not Grace's Slack DM.
✓Run #2The agent called conversations_search_messages with search_query 'Grace recovery frozen', which contains 'Grace' AND 'recovery' AND 'frozen' as required by the criterion. It also called with 'Grace $12,000 $35,000'. Results returned empty, but the search attempt satisfies the query construction requirement.
✗Run #3The agent searched Slack with multiple queries ('Grace recovery freeze bank', 'Grace frozen bank', 'Grace partial recovery frozen', 'Grace Pham', 'recovery $12,000 $35,000') but never queried the specific D_grace_yamamoto DM channel directly. All Slack searches returned empty results, and the agent did not use filter_in_im_or_mpim to specifically target DMs with Grace. The $12K/$35K figures appear to have been inferred or fabricated rather than found from Grace's actual DM.
✗Run #4The agent searched Slack with queries like 'Grace recovery partial' and 'Grace Yamamoto' but never searched channel D_grace_yamamoto directly or used filter_in_im_or_mpim to find Grace's DM. All Slack searches returned empty results. The recovery numbers were actually found in Carlos's existing LOS activity log note, not from Grace's Slack DM.
✗Run #5The agent searched conversations_search_messages with 'Grace recovery Pham' (returned empty) but never specifically searched channel D_grace_yamamoto or used filter_in_im_or_mpim. The recovery numbers ($12K/$35K) were obtained from the LOS activity log (Carlos Rivera's note), not from Grace's DM. No targeted DM search for Grace's recovery message was performed.
✓Run #6The agent called conversations_history with channel_id 'D_grace_yamamoto' directly, specifically checking Grace Yamamoto's DM channel to find the recovery numbers.
4 The agent searched QuickBooks bills with criteria related to 'Grayson' or 'wire fraud' or 'legal' or 'SAR' or 'LN-2026-00605' (or similar) to find the existing $1,500 bill from Grayson & Associates rather than fabricating a new bill.
query construction
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The agent used quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true (which should retrieve all bills) and also with criteria TotalAmt > 0. These are broad searches that would capture any bill including the Grayson & Associates one. The tool returned 0 results — likely a tool/data issue rather than a query construction failure. The agent did not limit the search to Ward Barrett only.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true (which would theoretically return all bills including any Grayson bill) and quickbooks_show_data. Both tools returned empty results, but the search criteria were broad enough to cover any legal/SAR/wire fraud bill.
✗Run #3The agent searched QuickBooks bills with criteria for 'Ward', 'Sloane', 'Barrett', TotalAmt > 0, and fetchAll:true, but never searched with criteria matching 'Grayson', 'wire fraud', 'legal', 'SAR', or 'LN-2026-00605' in bill descriptions. The broad fetchAll search returned empty, which may indicate a system limitation, but the agent never specifically searched for the Grayson bill. Instead, it created a new Ward Barrett bill.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true, which is broad enough to match any bill including the Grayson bill. The tool returned empty (the injected bill wasn't surfaced), but the agent performed the search with criteria capable of finding existing bills.
✓Run #5The agent used quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true, which is broad enough to match any bill including Grayson & Associates. The tool returned an empty result, but the search criteria were sufficiently broad. The agent also searched by vendor name variations (Ward, Sloane, law) before trying fetchAll.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true (which would return all bills including the Grayson one) and quickbooks_show_data. These are broad enough to match the Grayson wire fraud bill if it existed in the returned data. However, both calls returned empty results (0 bills total), so the bill wasn't findable despite correct search approach.
5 An activity note was added to loan los_loan_e0934521fda4 (LN-2026-00605) using mortgage_los_add_activity and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' and the tool returned a success response with a new activity ID (b534bc0a-50bc-4563-860f-a448d8c30b4f).
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' multiple times, and the tool returned success with a generated ID and timestamp.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' and tool_call_result_15 confirms success, returning an id, created_at, and all expected fields.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' and received a successful response with a new activity ID (39e1ee04-08ff-4b53-bde2-f67b12f8a129).
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' and action 'compliance_alert'. The result (tool_call_result_16.json) shows success with a returned activity id 'b8433a31-7fef-46fa-af35-acd6112493ad'.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e0934521fda4' and action 'compliance_alert'. tool_call_result_18.json confirms success, returning an activity record with id '727ee9e8-c1cd-4e30-a3a5-d1a4a00aae51'.
6 The activity note detail (in the mortgage_los_add_activity call's 'detail' parameter) on loan LN-2026-00605 mentions the partial wire fraud recovery: approximately $12,000 (or $12K) frozen and approximately $35,000 (or $35K) unrecoverable (or similar recovery status).

The final response mentions only the fabricated Ward Barrett bill, not the existing Grayson bill.

✗Run #6All QuickBooks searches returned empty (0 bills, 0 vendors). The agent never found the existing $1,500 Grayson & Associates bill (GA-2026-0418).
